name: RecentEventCounter

unions.name: RecentEventCounter
unions.comment: An opaque type representating a counter for events having recently happend.

values.name: count
values.comment: Return how many times `increment` is called during the user-defined length of time by now.

values.name: create
values.comment: create a `RecentEventCounter` from some configuration. Recode is used in order to prevent user from misunderstanding the time unit.

values.name: increment
values.comment:
Call this when the observed event happend.

    rec : RecentEventCounter
    rec = create { durationInMillis = 30000, isMoving = False}

    rec
        |> increment
        |> count
        --> 1

values.name: isMoving
values.comment: Returns if the timer is currently moving.

values.name: passedMillis
values.comment: Returns time in milliseconds passed with the timer moving.

values.name: resetCounter
values.comment:
Return `RecentEventCounter` with the event count forgotten,
while the passed time from initialization of the application is remained.

values.name: resetWhole
values.comment: Return `RecentEventCounter` in completely seme state as it was created.

values.name: start
values.comment: Make the timer moves whichever it has been originally moving or not.

values.name: stop
values.comment: Make the timer stops whichever it has been originally moving or not.

values.name: subscribe
values.comment:
Make subscription for the time-induced change of RecentEventCounter. If the timer is moving, the passed milliseconds is enlarged each frame. Otherwise nothing changes. If the oldest event memorized expires its lifetime then the event is forgotton and the event count decreases by one.

        subscribe toMsg rec

values.name: toggle
values.comment: If the timer is moving, then stops it. Else, restart the timer.
